chief_complaint: A 9-year-old male patient presented with fever, symptoms of a viral upper respiratory infection, and tachycardia.
history: The patient had a history of otitis media that, if left undiagnosed or untreated, can lead to meningitis. He presented with fever, symptoms of a viral upper respiratory infection, and tachycardia. His physical examination was remarkable for muscle hypotonia, asymmetrical pupils, and incomprehensible speech.
exam: Vital signs: tachycardia. Physical examination: muscle hypotonia, asymmetrical pupils, incomprehensible speech.
labs: Laboratory testing and imaging confirmed Streptococcus pneumoniae meningitis, acute mastoiditis, and hydrocephalus.
